Document:
#High Roleplay Roles
Some roles have a major impact on the round and therefore require a higher level of roleplay.
##Colony Command Roles
A person with a "colony command role" is defined as anyone currently in one of the following positions:
- The Colony Administrator
- Colony Operations Synthetics
- Colony Law Enforcement Leaders (CMB Marshal, etc)
Colony Command must attempt to follow Colony Law where applicable, and only in extreme cases of military miscarriage of justice may mutiny and rebel against them.
Colony Command may not willingly take any action that would end in the destruction of the colony or the mass harm of its people.
Those in colony command roles must be able to communicate clearly and concisely in English when communicating on radio channels and announcements, and while in any professional setting.
##Military Command Roles
A person with a "military command role" is defined as anyone currently in one of the following positions:
- The Commander.
- The Adjutant Officer/Executive Officer.
- Staff Officers (CMO, CMP, LO)
- Any officer (WO1+) in the position of Acting Commander.
- Non-Combat Synthetics (Auxiliary Support, etc).
- Any role holding a rank O3 or above (usually a captain) or equivalent.
Military Command must follow the Uniform Code of Military Justice and the Standard Operating Procedures, except if they are part of a valid mutiny (a mutiny is only valid if you have received permission from a Moderator).
Military Command may not willingly take any action that would end in the destruction of their ship/installation or the mass harm of allied military members unless the action would reasonably end in faction mechanical victory (reference the objectives console) or the saving of multiple civilian lives.
Military Command may not deploy to the Area of Operations if it would leave the CIC unstaffed; there must always be at least one person in the CIC at all times.
Those in military command roles must be able to communicate clearly and concisely in English when communicating on radio channels and announcements, and while in any professional setting.
##CLF Cell Leader
As the CLF Cell Leader, you are in command of your entire Cell. You are held to much higher standards than the other members of your cell and will be punished more severely in the event of rule breaches.
The CLF’s goal is to force the withdrawal of the government forces from the colony; you are not a terrorist organisation but a militia force.
Do not reference memes or break character; you are expected to roleplay to a higher standard than others.
Only harm civilians if they are hostile to your cause. The goal of your cause should be to recruit civilians, not to harm them or kill them without a reason. Harming or killing civilians puts you at risk of being discovered far more easily, as well as making recruiting almost impossible.
For example, during a scouting mission, a civilian starts reporting on the colony radio about armed insurgent forces within the colony; this would count as them being hostile towards you.
##The Xenomorph Queen
As the Queen, you are in command of the whole Hive. You have higher standards than other Xenomorphs, and will be punished more severely in case of a rule breach - do not reference memes, do not speak jokingly to the point of fail RP and do not draw unrelated things in the mind map aside from tactical decisions/orders.
The Queen may use her banishment tool on any Xenomorph that fails to follow the rules set above. The banish tool is not to be used lightly or as a joke - only as a punishment. The Queen may not lock out, force to play or banish any castes - drone tax is prohibited. She may, however, request specific castes, given they provide some needed tactical advantage, such as requesting a Boiler in case the military has weak acid protection or requesting a Burrower for the lack of tunnels in the map and may devolve unruly Xenomorphs accordingly.
If unsure, ahelp for game admin permission. The Queen has human-level intelligence and may give direct orders to her offspring to kill any suspicious synthetic units even if they are not provoking directly (such as by walking into weeded territory or being close to military force) or to not attack any hosts, provided they have a reason (such as forcing a host to drive the dropship back to the main ship).
##Synthetic Units
Synthetics are artificial humanoids initially invented by the Weyland Corporation, akin to the sci-fi android trope. It has gone through several iterations and has countless specifications, but one hasn't changed. Synthetics are embedded with core directives that guide their every move and their being.
By playing a synthetic character, you agree to have read and to abide by the rules written in this section.
Core directives apply to all synthetics. Non-Combat (NC) Directives apply only to NC synthetics, and the Combat Directives apply only to combat synthetics and rogue / hijacked synthetics.
- Key Expectations
Chain of Command: Synthetic ranks only grant respect - they do not have any actual authority to provide orders unless stated otherwise.
Role Atmosphere: As a High-Roleplay Role, you are expected to roleplay with the seriousness required. Do not wear mismatched/silly clothing (as traffic cones on the head slot), do not act in a silly manner and do not be aggressive or otherwise go against the spirit of the role.
- Core Directives
Prioritise Self-Preservation: Avoid actions that endanger your survival, for example, by escaping entrapment or agreeing to risky tasks when critically damaged. In combat, ensure the threat is neutralised or disengaged. Pursue briefly only to prevent their return and facilitate disengagement. Refer to the self-preservation section. Avoid conflict; if attacked, defend yourself. Always assess risk and withdraw if you have no chance of survival. Heed warnings, no matter how empty they may be, and avoid needless danger. Always use judgment to evaluate your actions to avoid violating self-preservation. This directive takes precedence over any other.
Do what is best for [Faction]: Adhere fully to your faction’s laws (e.g., Uniform Code of Military Justice as GOVFOR) and lawful orders from Command. Honour valid crew requests (e.g., reviving or retrieving a dead ally) unless the task is impossible, less urgent, inefficient or violates programming. Do not harm faction members or insult them.
- Non-Combat Directives
NC synthetics are restricted from active combat and must follow these guidelines.
You may not use any sort of weapons (for example, firearms, combat knives, emplaced weapons (think of a mortar or machine gun) or improvised weaponry). You may not utilise grenades or explosives of any kind in any capacity, unless for demolition purposes in a non-combat area; you may throw live grenades away from yourself / friendlies. Sentry Turrets may be set up on defensive points, such as the FOB, but may never be set up near a combat area / the frontline. Non-lethal engagement, such as tackling or tasers, may be used defensively or to subdue immediate threats only.
Adopt a defensive posture, tend to the wounded, limit your engagements as much as possible, avoid "tug-of-war" situations and aid your faction with support in auxiliary areas. Focus on threat removal, and not elimination.
NC Synthetics are expected to not perform non life threathenting surgery on the frontline; they may only fix IBs and Organs freely; otherwise, all non-life-threatening issues should be fixed within an enclosed caded area, FOB or the ship/installation.
- Combat Directives
Combat synthetics and rogue synthetics are not bound to any kind of behavioural inhibitor and may engage in combat at any given moment they deem necessary/beneficial just as a human would do. You may utilise any sort of weapon, explosives, or otherwise, and may engage in active combat even if risky. Your self-preservation directives are more relaxed, meaning you may engage in riskier situations more often. You are not bound to auxiliary roles, and even if able, you may decline superior orders to aid in supportive areas if you deem those inefficient. You are still bound to your faction's directives and must follow any superior orders to the best of your capacities as any other synthetic unit would do.
- Working Joes
As a Gen 1 Synthetic, your problem-solving capabilities are extremely primitive, just sufficient for basic manual labour and low-level maintenance activities. Working Joes have no personality, opinions or unique views that would set one apart from another. You may not perform advanced tasks (building, reviving/healing, etc) as that is outside of your skillset. You should never heal yourself, and instead request repair from other, higher-generation synthetic units or human technicians. Do not utilise the radio unless in an absolute emergency, and instead utilise the phones to speak with other departments/buildings in your ship/colony/faction. Never perform any Joe-to-Joe communication except for the AI Radio channel. Identify yourself as “UNIT ####” (e.g., UNIT 0234, trespasser on AI Core identified)
Avoid speaking in-person using the local IC chat and instead utilise your voice lines for communication with other non-Joe players. Only speak in the chat if your voice lines won't suffice, and it is something urgent / too specific, and try to maintain the Joe atmosphere while at it.
Working Joes working for a military ship/installation are permitted to change their regular attire for - Enlisted Faction Uniform - Faction Engineering Belt - Faction Engineering backpack - Faction Footwear
Working Joes are not permitted to wear Hats, Masks, Ear accessories or neck accessories of any kind, such as pins and scarves.
Working Joes have almost no sense of self-preservation. Even if attacked, do not react greatly - throw in one or two punches to scare off, but allow yourself to be greatly harmed/destroyed. Be unbothered by any kind of violence going on around you and do not directly aid in any capacity aside from reporting the situation (e.g. calling over medical on a dead human).
If you end up in a grid different from your AI Core, stay motionless until brought back (e.g., a Colony Working Joe being brought up to the Orbital Station through a dropship should remain motionless until they are brought back to the colony).
